heading: Improvement of education in technical fields: general authority regarding education in science, mathematics, and engineering

Department of Defense Support for Science, Mathematics, and Engineering Education, , , directed Secretary of Defense to develop and submit to Congress a master plan for activities by Department of Defense during each of fiscal years 1993 through 1997 to support education in science, mathematics, and engineering at all levels of education in the United States, with each such plan to be developed in consultation with Secretary of Education, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. L. 102–190, div. A, title VIII, § 829105 Stat. 1444Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title X, § 1063(c)110 Stat. 444Dec. 5, 1991Feb. 10, 1996

children.text: identify actions which the Department of Defense may take to improve education in the scientific, mathematics, and engineering skills necessary to meet the long-term national defense needs of the United States for personnel proficient in such skills; and
establish and conduct programs to carry out such actions.

children.label: A
children.text: enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with eligible entities;

children.label: B
children.text: make grants of financial assistance to eligible entities;

children.label: C
children.text: provide cash awards and other items to eligible entities;

children.label: D
children.text: accept voluntary services from eligible entities; and

children.label: E
children.text: support national competition judging, other educational event activities, and associated award ceremonies in connection with these educational programs.
The Secretary of Defense may carry out the authority in paragraph (1) through the Secretaries of the military departments.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “eligible entity” includes a department or agency of the Federal Government, a State, a political subdivision of a State, an individual, and a not-for-profit or other organization in the private sector.

children.label: B
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: c
children.text: The Secretary shall designate an individual within the Office of the Secretary of Defense to advise and assist the Secretary regarding matters relating to science, mathematics, and engineering education and training.
